free_text: Yields of Potassium Isotopes in High Energy Bombardment
Of Vanadium, Iron, Cobalt, Nickel, Copper and Zinc.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
Targets Materials of V,Fe,Co,Ni and Cu were Available
As Spectroscopically Pure Foils with Superficial
Densities Randing Between 22.05 mg/cm**2 (NICKEL)And
81.4 mg/cm**2 (IRON).Somewhat Thicker Zinc Foils
(150 mg/cm**2) Were Made by Melting a.R. Grade Zinc
Under Zinc Chloride and Hammering and Rolling the
Resulting Button of Zinc.
Beta-Proportional Counter.
3-In.*3-In.  Sodium Iodide Crystal.
At 590 MeV.
At 18.3 GeV.
It was Shown Experimentally That the Chemical
Procedure Removed Sodium Contamination from Potassium
Projected Into the Aluminium Foils.
The Standard Error on the Results is Conservatively
Estimated at +-4%.
- By Compiler - Ground State of K-38 can not be
Produced Due to Radioactive Decay of Ca-38 and Isomer
State of K-38.
After Fast Chemical Separation.
For the K-38/K-42 Ratio an Appreciable Additional
Error Arises Because of the Extrapolation Over Ten
Half-Lives and the Results Cannot be Significant to
Better Than +-20% Although Their Internal Consistency
Is Better Than this.
After Fast Chemical Separation)
/by Authors/.Ratios of Yields of K-43 to K-42 were
Calculated Both from the Isotope-Separator Results and
The Decay Curve Analysis.Both these Results have Been
Estimated Deviations of +-3%.
.From Both Decay Curves Resolution.  For R Only
.From the Isotope-Separator Experiments. For R Only
K-44 Production Due to Radioactive Decay of Ar-44
(T1/2=11.87 MIN) Could not be Excluded.
After Fast Chemical Separation.
/by Authors/.The Values For K-44 to K-42 are Less
Reliable Because of Poorer Counting Statistics and the
Limits are Set at About +-10%.
